mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate cut periods, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, labor market deterioration signals severe demand contraction and recession risk, causing investors to price in negative earnings revisions and driving S&P 500 returns lower over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 8.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in post-2009 data.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer spending and construction activity, generating positive earnings momentum that lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and pushes S&P 500 returns higher over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns turn negative in over 50% of periods when INDPRO increases, or the signal reverses direction during late-cycle phases without regime adjustment.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real GDP growth confirm macroeconomic expansion, reinforcing corporate revenue expectations and generating sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 changes remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows, or 60-day forward returns show no directional bias during GDP expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4% monthly triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and driving S&P 500 returns negative over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 55% of high-inflation months, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and corporate hiring capacity to drive positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal manufacturing contraction and supply chain bottlenecks, prompting risk-off positioning that drives S&P 500 returns negative over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of consecutive decline periods, or the signal fails to predict direction during early-cycle recovery phases.
